name: St. Pius X Church
description_short: St. Pius X Church Federal Credit Union is a faith-based credit union established in 1967 serving Rochester, NY with $94.2M in assets and 5,600+ members.
description_long:
St. Pius X Church Federal Credit Union was established in 1967 and is headquartered in Rochester, New York. As a faith-based financial institution, it specifically serves multiple religious communities and faith-based organizations with tailored financial products aligned with their values. The credit union operates a single branch location at 2998 Chili Avenue and manages over $94.2 million in assets with a dedicated staff of 15+ employees serving its membership base.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of banking and lending products including checking accounts (Share Draft), savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), credit cards, personal loans, auto loans (new and used vehicles), and mortgage options (fixed-rate and adjustable-rate mortgages). Members can access online banking, mobile banking apps for iOS and Android, bill pay, e-statements, remote deposit capture, mobile payments, and electronic signature authentication. Financial calculators are available for mortgage, auto loan, and credit card payoff planning.

What distinguishes St. Pius X Church FCU is its explicit focus on faith-based membership and religious community orientation. The institution has cultivated a loyal membership base with specific account products: 6,100+ regular savings accounts, 2,400+ share draft accounts, 400+ money market accounts, and 1,100+ share certificates. The credit union emphasizes community engagement through its online Q&A forum and maintains transparent service status reporting for online banking, mobile app, website, phone, and ATM services.

As a single-branch credit union with limited physical accessibility (1 branch, 1 ATM), St. Pius X Church FCU may present challenges for members seeking in-person services outside regular hours or those preferring wider branch networks. The institution's membership eligibility restricted to faith-based organizations and religious communities limits accessibility compared to broader-membership credit unions. However, for faith-based organizations and their congregants seeking alignment between financial services and religious values, the credit union provides a specialized alternative to secular banking institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online banking with bill pay and e-statements
pros: Faith-based mission explicitly aligned with religious community values
Comprehensive lending products including mortgages (15-year, adjustable-rate options) and auto loans
Mobile banking apps available for both iOS and Android devices
Strong asset base of $94.2 million with stable 5,600+ member following
Online banking with bill pay, remote deposit capture, and mobile payments
Established 1967 with 56+ years of operations and community presence
Transparent service status reporting and community Q&A support
cons: Only 1 physical branch location limits accessibility and convenience for members
Only 1 ATM available restricts cash access compared to larger credit unions
Membership eligibility restricted to faith-based organizations, not open to general public
No reviews currently available on the platform, limiting member feedback transparency
Limited branch network may discourage members relocating outside Rochester area
best_for: Members of faith-based organizations and religious congregations seeking values-aligned banking
Rochester, NY residents and employees of St. Pius X Church and affiliated religious communities
Individuals seeking personal and auto loans with a community-oriented, faith-based lender
Religious organizations needing business banking and financial services aligned with mission
diagnosis: St. Pius X Church FCU is best for members of faith-based organizations in the Rochester area who prioritize values-aligned banking and can work with a single-branch, limited-ATM network. The primary caveat is restricted membership eligibility to faith-based communities and geographic limitations due to minimal physical presence.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
